g.onclick: sendPrompt('How do I design a BPF hash map to track TCP connection state per pid+fd key?')
sendPrompt('How do I track per-fd in-flight HTTP request timestamps across write() and read() probes?')
sendPrompt('What is the maximum safe size to copy from a user-space buffer in an eBPF program?')
sendPrompt('How do I capture TCP connect and close events into a BPF ring buffer?')
sendPrompt('How do I implement a connection tracker in Go that keys connections by pid and fd?')
sendPrompt('How do I parse partial HTTP/1.1 request and response headers from a byte buffer in Go?')
sendPrompt('How do I construct an OTLP trace span from HTTP request and response data in Go?')
sendPrompt('How do I emit a completed OTLP span with the Go OTel SDK using explicit start and end times?')

svg.text: key insight: {pid, fd} is unique per connection at any instant — fd is reused after close(),
so conn_events OPEN/CLOSE bracket the lifetime and prevent stale-fd collisions
